channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇷 Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi in Critical Condition in Iranian Hospital
item.description: Jailed Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi remains in critical condition in a cardiac care unit in northwestern Iran after being transferred from prison. Her family foundation reports her health is deteriorating due to her continued imprisonment.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Four Injured in South London Shooting
item.description: Four people were hospitalized with gunshot wounds in a shooting in south London. Police are investigating whether the incident is related to a stabbing that occurred the same day.

item.title: 🇪🇺 ECB Policymaker Warns Euro Zone Recession Fears Are Real
item.description: Bank of Greece governor Yannis Stournaras warned that euro zone recession concerns are "real and justified" as Middle East conflict continues. He emphasized that talks to end the Iran war will be critical for ECB monetary policy decisions.

item.title: 🌏 Asian Development Bank Launches Critical Minerals Finance Scheme
item.description: The ADB launched a new financing initiative to help Asia-Pacific countries develop supply chains for critical minerals used in clean energy, batteries, and electric vehicles. The move addresses growing demand for strategic resources.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Pays Steep Price in Battle to Reverse Fortunes
item.description: Israel has displaced hundreds of thousands of Hezbollah constituents and killed as many as several thousand of its fighters. Domestic rivals are hardening opposition against Hezbollah status as an armed group.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Swedish Coast Guard Seizes Suspected False-Flag Tanker in Baltic Sea
item.description: Swedish authorities seized a tanker suspected of being part of Russia's shadow fleet operating under false flags in the Baltic Sea. The incident highlights continued tensions in the region and enforcement of sanctions.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Administration Halts US Wind Farm Projects Citing National Security
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has brought U.S. onshore wind development to a halt, citing national security concerns. The Financial Times reported the move represents a significant shift in energy policy.

item.title: 🇵🇪 Peru Electoral Board Calls for Audit of Election Results
item.description: Peru's National Jury of Elections requested a "comprehensive and exhaustive IT audit" of the April 12 general election results. The move raises questions about the integrity of the electoral process.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Court Extends Detention of Gaza Flotilla Activists
item.description: An Israeli court extended by two days the detention of two activists arrested aboard a Gaza-bound flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters near Greece. The flotilla was attempting to break the Gaza blockade.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Launches Drone Strikes on Russia's Primorsk Port
item.description: Ukraine launched a wave of drone strikes targeting Russia's Primorsk port. Russia and Ukraine continue to exchange cross-border attacks as the war enters its latest phase.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran-US War: Trump Reviews Peace Proposal as Deadline Passes
item.description: President Trump is reviewing a new 14-point peace proposal from Iran but signaled he is likely to reject it, saying Iran has "not yet paid a big enough price." The congressional authorization deadline has passed.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Israel Conducts Operations in West Bank Near Nablus
item.description: Israeli forces conducted operations in the West Bank near Nablus amid continued tensions in the occupied territories. The operations are part of ongoing security efforts in the region.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China and Philippines Trade Accusations Over South China Sea Incidents
item.description: China and the Philippines exchanged accusations over South China Sea activities. Both sides alleged illegal landings and covert research operations in disputed waters, continuing tensions in the strategic waterway.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Approves Plan to Buy F-35 and F-15I Aircraft from US
item.description: Israel approved a major defense purchase plan to acquire F-35 and F-15I aircraft from the United States. The deal strengthens Israel's air superiority capabilities amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Offers Strait Deal but Trump Dissatisfied, Prefers Non-Military Path
item.description: Iran offered a Strait of Hormuz deal but Trump expressed dissatisfaction, saying Iran had not yet paid enough of a price. He publicly mused about the possibility of restarting airstrikes.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia-Ukraine Give Conflicting Accounts of Village in Sumy Region
item.description: Russia's Defence Ministry claimed its troops took control of Myropillia in Ukraine's Sumy region, but Ukraine's military denied the village had changed hands. The conflicting accounts reflect continued fighting.

item.title: 🇸🇰 Slovakia's PM Fico to Attend Victory Day Parade in Moscow
item.description: Slovakian Prime Minister Robert Fico will attend Russia's Victory Day parade in Moscow, according to Russian state television. The attendance signals continued ties between Slovakia and Russia despite EU tensions.

item.title: 🇻🇦 US Secretary of State Rubio to Meet Pope Leo This Week
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is expected to travel to Italy this week to meet with Pope Leo. The visit comes weeks after a clash between Donald Trump and the pontiff drew criticism across the political spectrum.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Two Women Die in Channel Crossing Attempt Between France and Britain
item.description: Two women died while attempting to cross the English Channel from France to Britain. The incident highlights the continued dangers faced by migrants attempting the crossing.

item.title: 🇯🇵 SoftBank Aims to Make Data Center Batteries Without Lithium or Cobalt
item.description: SoftBank aims to develop data center batteries without using lithium or cobalt, addressing supply chain concerns and environmental issues related to critical mineral sourcing.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Comey Prosecution Makes No Sense, Says Senator Tillis
item.description: Senator Thom Tillis criticized the prosecution of former FBI Director James Comey, saying it made no sense. The comments reflect ongoing debate about the Justice Department's approach to political figures.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Defense Forces Report on Gaza Flotilla Interception
item.description: The Jerusalem Post reported on Israel's interception of the Gaza-bound flotilla and subsequent detention of activists. The flotilla was stopped in international waters near Greece.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran-US War Live Updates: Trump Reviews Ceasefire, Germany on Strait
item.description: Ongoing coverage of the Iran-US conflict including ceasefire negotiations, Trump's response to Iranian proposals, and German involvement in Strait of Hormuz security discussions.

item.title: 🇵🇭 South China Sea Tensions: China-Philippines Trade Accusations
item.description: China and the Philippines traded accusations over South China Sea activities, including allegations of illegal landings and covert research operations. Tensions continue in disputed waters.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Iran Proposes 30-Day End to War, Trump Likely to Reject
item.description: Iran proposed an end to the war within 30 days, but Trump signaled he is unlikely to accept the deal, saying Iran had "not yet paid a big enough price" and publicly mused about restarting airstrikes.
